==Further reading==
===Scholarly studies===
* Altman, Stuart, and David Shactman. ''Power, Politics, and Universal Health Care: The Inside Story of a Century-Long Battle'' (2011) [https://www.google.com/books/edition/Power_Politics_and_Universal_Health_Care/zVBjKdBo2ioC?hl=en online] 
*Armenian;  Haroutune K., and Sam Shapiro, eds. ''Epidemiology and Health Services'' (1997) [https://archive.org/details/epidemiologyheal0000haro/mode/2up online]
* Barr, Donald A. ''Introduction to US health policy: The organization, financing, and delivery of health care in America'' (2023) [https://books.google.com/books?hl=en&lr=&id=PvnKEAAAQBAJ&oi=fnd&pg=PP1&dq=%5BBOOK%5D+Introduction+to+US+health+policy:+The+organization,+financing,+and+delivery+of+health+care+in+America+DA+Barr+-+2023+-+books.google.com&ots=hfBFSo-3aS&sig=6hVhj99lROcCbRI40YzLOtzFZwM excerpt]
* Blake, John B. "The origins of public health in the United States."  ''American Journal of Public Health and the Nations Health'' 38.11 (1948): pp.&nbsp;1539–1550. [https://ajph.aphapublications.org/doi/pdf/10.2105/AJPH.38.11.1539 online]
* Blumenthal, David, and James A. Morone. ''The heart of power : health and politics in the Oval Office'' (2010) [https://www.google.com/books/edition/The_Heart_of_Power/UEGLO9YUecEC?hl=en online]
* Bordley, James, and A. McGehee Harvey. ''Two centuries of American medicine, 1776-1976'' (1976). [https://archive.org/details/twocenturiesofam0000bord/page/n7/mode/2up online]
* Brandt, Allan M., and Martha Gardner. "Antagonism and accommodation: interpreting the relationship between public health and medicine in the United States during the 20th century." ''American Journal of Public Health'' 90.5 (2000): 707+. [https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC1446218/pdf/10800418.pdf online]
* Burnham, J. C. ''Health Care in America: A History'' (Johns Hopkins UP, 2015), a standard comprehensive scholarly history; [https://archive.org/details/healthcareinamer0000burn/page/n5/mode/2up online].
* Byrd, W.M. and L.A. Clayton. ''An American health dilemma: A medical history of African Americans and the problem of race: Beginnings to 1900'' (Routledge, 2012).
* Carney, Jan K. ''Controversies in public health and health policy'' (2016) [https://archive.org/details/controversiesinp0000carn/page/n5/mode/2up online]
* Carney, Jan K. ''A History of Public Health: From Past to Present'' (2022)  [https://books.google.com/books?hl=en&lr=&id=d6qgEAAAQBAJ&oi=fnd&pg=PP1&dq=+Carney,+Jan+K.+%22past+to+present%22&ots=BN2msjaCBL&sig=Gqequu6ckR3GICY_C892sAaW_n8 online]
* Christou, Stella. "Social movements in healthcare: learning for leaders." ''Research Handbook on Leadership in Healthcare. '' (Edward Elgar, 2023) pp.&nbsp;665–685.
* Cutler, David, and Grant Miller. "The role of public health improvements in health advances: the twentieth-century United States." ''Demography'' 42.1 (2005): 1–22. [https://www.nber.org/system/files/working_papers/w10511/w10511.pdf online]
* Deutsch, A. ''The Mentally Ill in America: A History of Their Care and Treatment from Colonial Times'' (1937).
* Duffy, John. ''Epidemics in Colonial America'' (1953) [https://archive.org/details/colonial-epidemics-in-colonial-america/page/n3/mode/1up online]
* Duffy, John. ''The Healers: A History of American Medicine'' (U of Illinois Press, 1976) [https://archive.org/details/healershistoryof0000duff online]
* Duffy, John. ''The sanitarians : a history of American public health'' (1992) [https://archive.org/details/sanitarianshisto0000duff_h1v9/mode/1up online]
* Friedberger, Mark. "The Decision To Institutionalize: Families With Exceptional Children in 1900" ''Journal of family history. '' (1981) 6(4), p.&nbsp;396-409 DOI: 10.1177/036319908100600403 on feeble-minded.
* Freymann, John Gordon. "Medicine's Great Schism: Prevention vs. Cure: An Historical Interpretation," ''Medical Care'', Vol. 13, No. 7 (1975), pp.&nbsp;525–536 [http://www.jstor.org/stable/3763301 online], on the history of public health research.
*Gerl, Ellen. " 'Out of the Back Rooms' Physician-publicist Virginia Apgar Makes Birth Defects a Popular Cause." ''Journalism History'' 42.3 (2016): 122-129 [http://www.ellengerl.com/wp-content/uploads/2012/10/Gerl_JH_October2016_Apgar.pdf online]
* Grob, Gerald N. ''Mental illness and American society, 1875-1940'' (Princeton UP, 2019). [https://books.google.com/books?hl=en&lr=&id=X1mYDwAAQBAJ&oi=fnd&pg=PR8&dq=grob+mental+illness&ots=DuzdJV161f&sig=7ZSKPQpjKGzNBH8EcjET7-SjqvI online]
* Grob, Gerald N.  ''From asylum to community: Mental health policy in modern America'' (Princeton UP, 2014) [https://books.google.com/books?hl=en&lr=&id=8DgABAAAQBAJ&oi=fnd&pg=PP1&dq=From+asylum+to+community+Grob&ots=_qm-FMI293&sig=zS3EmOwj5d-dwjveA0SsVnJJjpU online]
* Hall, Courtney R. " The rise of professional surgery in the United States. 1800–1865". ''Bulletin of the History of Medicine'' (1952) 26#3 pp.&nbsp;231–262 [https://www.jstor.org/stable/44446398 online]
* Harrison, Gordon A. ''Mosquitoes, malaria, and man: A history of the hostilities since 1880'' (1978) [https://archive.org/details/mosquitoesmalari00harr/page/n4/mode/1up online]
* Hollingsworth, J. Rogers, Jerald Hage, and Robert Hanneman. ''State intervention in medical care: consequences for Britain, France, Sweden, and the United States, 1890-1970'' (Cornell UP, 2019).
* Hollingsworth, J. Rogers. ''A Political Economy of Medicine: Great Britain and the United States'' (Johns Hopkins UP, 1986), covers 1890 to 1970.
* Kalisch, Philip Arthur, and Beatrice J. Kalisch. ''The advance of American Nursing'' (3rd ed 1996) [https://archive.org/details/advanceofamerica03edkali online], A standard scholarly history.
* Leavitt. Judith W. and R.L. Numbers, eds. ''Sickness and health in America: Readings in the history of medicine and public health'' (3rd ed. 1997).
* Lerner, Monroe, and Odin W. Anderson. ''Health progress in the United States, 1900–1960'' (1963) [https://archive.org/details/healthprogressin0000lern/page/n8/mode/1up online]
* Loving, David A.  "The development of American public health, 1850–1925" (PhD dissertation, U of Oklahoma; ProQuest Dissertations & Theses,  2008. 3303520).
* McBride, David. ''Caring for Equality: A History of African American Health and Healthcare'' (Rowman and Littlefield, 2018).
* Mathis, Adria D. "Measles update—United States, january 1–april 17, 2025." ''MMWR. Morbidity and Mortality Weekly Report'' 74 (2025).
* Meckel, Richard A. ''Save the Babies: American Public Health Reform and the Prevention of Infant Mortality, 1850-1929'' (Johns Hopkins UP, 1990).
* Mohr, James C. ''Doctors and the law: medical jurisprudence in nineteenth-century America'' (1993) [https://archive.org/details/doctorslawmedica0000mohr/page/n6/mode/1up online]
* Mohr, James C. ''Abortion in America : the origins and evolution of national policy, 1800-1900'' (1978) [https://archive.org/details/abortioninameric00jame online]
* Mukherjee, Siddhartha. ''The Emperor of All Maladies: A Biography of Cancer'' (2010); 585 pp.: scholarly history
* Nathanson, Constance A. ''Disease prevention as Social Change: The State, Society, and Public Health in the United States, France, Great Britain, and Canada'' (Russell Sage Foundation, 2007).
* Oettinger, Katherine B. "Title V of the Social Security Act: What it has meant to children." ''Social Security Bulletin'' 23 (1960): 39–50. [https://www.ssa.gov/policy/docs/ssb/v23n8/v23n8p39.pdf online]
* Olivarius, Kathryn. "How We Died: Public and Private Health in Early America" ''William and Mary Quarterly'' 81#1 (2024)  pp.&nbsp;147–154  https://doi.org/10.1353/wmq.2024.a918184
* Oshinsky, David M. ''Polio: an American story'' (Oxford UP, 2005) [https://books.google.com/books?hl=en&lr=&id=cTliwSU62KIC&oi=fnd&pg=PR7&dq=salk+oshinsky&ots=-pUNpwhpX1&sig=zvs_ShNm0hjrAEH5SFTy6EdbLCc excerpt], Pulitzer Prize.
* Ott, Katherine. ''Fevered Lives: Tuberculosis in American Culture Since 1870'' (Harvard UP, 1996) [https://www.google.com/books/edition/Fevered_Lives/OyX3aaRX1-0C?hl=en online]
* Perdiguero, Enrique, et al. "History of health, a valuable tool in public health." ''Journal of Epidemiology & Community Health'' 55.9 (2001): 667–673. [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC1731976/pdf/v055p00667.pdf online]
* Petrzela, Natalia Mehlman. ''Fit Nation: The Gains and Pains of America's Exercise Obsession'' (2023)
* Quadagno, Jill. "Why the United States has no national health insurance: Stakeholder mobilization against the welfare state, 1945-1996." ''Journal of Health and Social Behavior'' (2004) v45 extra issue pp.&nbsp;25–44. [https://www.academia.edu/download/45312488/JHSB04ExtraQuadagno.pdf online]
* Ravenel, Mazÿck Porcher, ed. ''A half century of public health'' (1921); 19 topical essays by experts covering 1870–1920 in U.S.  [https://archive.org/details/in.ernet.dli.2015.168637/page/n257/mode/2up online]
* Rosen, George. ''A history of public health.'' (2nd ed. JHU Press, 2015), a major scholarly history with focus on Britain, Germany, France and the U.S.; [https://archive.org/details/historyofpublich0000geor/page/n6/mode/1up online].
* Rosenberg, Charles E., and Carroll S.Rosenberg. "Pietism and the Origins of the American Public Health Movement: A Note on John H. Griscom and Robert M. Hartley" ''Journal of the History of Medicine and Allied Sciences'' (1968) 23#1 (1968), pp.&nbsp;16–35 [https://www.jstor.org/stable/24621721 online]
* Rosenberg, Charles E. ''The cholera years; the United States in 1832, 1849, and 1866'' (U of Chicago Press 1962), In-depth study of epidemics.
* Rosner, David, and Gerald Markowitz. '' Building the Worlds That Kill Us: Disease, Death, and Inequality in American History'' (Columbia UP, 2024); argues disease was worsened by  capitalism, commercial greed, and social injustice.
* Rutkow, Ira M.  ''Bleeding Blue and Gray: Civil War Surgery and the Evolution of American Medicine'' (Random House, 2005)
* {{cite journal | last1 = Sahu | first1 = Hemlata | display-authors = etal | year = 2024 | title = Jonas Salk (1914-1995) Pioneering the Fight Against Polio and Beyond | journal = Cureus | volume = 16 | issue = 9| article-number = e69681 | pmid = 39429327 | doi = 10.7759/cureus.69681 | doi-access = free | pmc = 11489307 }}
* Schneider, Mary-Jane. ''Introduction to Public Health'' (6th edition, 2021) [https://samples.jblearning.com/9781284197594/9781284197709_FMxx_Schneider_Secured.pdf excerpt]
* Sedgwick, W. T. ''Principles of sanitary science and the public health : with special reference to the causation and prevention of infectious diseases'' (1922) [https://archive.org/details/principlesofsani00sedgrich/principlesofsani00sedgrich/page/n6/mode/1up online]
* Shapiro, Sam et al.. ''Infant, Perinatal, Maternal, and Childhood Mortality in the United States'' (Harvard UP, 1968) [https://archive.org/details/infantperinatalm0000sams online] ppp.223–267 on public health programs.
* Smith. Susan Lynn. ''Sick and Tired of Being Sick and Tired Black Women's Health Activism in America, 1890-1950'' (U Pennsylvania Press, 1995)
* Starr, Paul. ''The Social Transformation of American Medicine'' (Basic Books, 1982). very wide-ranging history of American medicine.
* Teller, Michael. ''The Tuberculosis Movement : A Public Health Campaign in the Progressive Era'' (1988)
** ''The American tuberculosis crusade 1889–1917: the rise of a modern health campaign'' (Ph D. Dissertation University of Chicago; ProQuest Dissertations & Theses,  1985. T-29462)
* Tomes, Nancy. "The private side of public health: sanitary science, domestic hygiene, and the germ theory, 1870-1900." ''Bulletin of the History of Medicine'' 64.4 (1990): 509–539. [https://www.jstor.org/stable/44443182 online]
* Tulchinsky, Theodore H., and Elena A. Varavikova. "A history of public health." ''The new public health'' (2014): 1-42 doi: 10.1016/B978-0-12-415766-8.00001-X, a major scholarly survey.
* Tyor, Peter L., and Jamil S. Zainaldin. "Asylum and Society: An Approach to Institutional Change." ''Journal of Social History''  13#1, 1979, pp.&nbsp;23–48. [http://www.jstor.org/stable/3786774 online]
* Ward, John W. and  Christian Warren, eds. ''Silent Victories: The history and practice of public health in Twentieth Century America'' (Oxford UP, 2007)  [https://archive.org/details/silentvictoriesh0000unse/mode/1up online]; Twenty two long scholarly articles. covering full range of. public health topics. in 20th century.
* Whorton, James C.  ''Crusaders for Fitness: The History of American Health Reformers'' (Princeton UP, 1982)
* Whorton, James C.  ''Nature Cures: The History of Alternative Medicine in America'' (2004)
* Wintermute, Bobby A. ''Public Health and the US Military : A History of the Army Medical Department, 1818-1917'' (Taylor & Francis, 2010) Online at Ebook Central.

=== Geography===
* Beach, Brian. "Water Infrastructure and Health in U.S. Cities" ''NBER Working Paper 28563'' (2021)  DOI 10.3386/w28563
* Blake, John B. ''Public Health in the Town of Boston: 1630-1822'' (Harvard University Press, 1959),
* Bonner, Thomas N. ''The Kansas Doctor: A Century of Pioneering'' (Kansas UP, 1959) pp 120–171, argues Kansas was a national leader in public health in 1904–1923.
* Bonner, Thomas N. ''Medicine in Chicago: 1850-1950'' (1957), pp.&nbsp;175–198.
* {{cite journal | last1 = Cheung | first1 = Philip C.W. | year = 2017 | title = A historical review of the benefits and hypothetical risks of disinfecting drinking water by chlorination (Updated and Revised) | url = | journal = Journal of Environment & Ecology | volume = 8 | issue = 1| pages = 73–145 | doi = 10.5296/jee.v8i1.11338 }}
* Coombs, Jan. "The Health of Central Wisconsin Residents in 1880: A New View of Midwestern Rural Life" ''Wisconsin Magazine of History'' 68#4 (1985), pp.&nbsp;284–311 [http://www.jstor.org/stable/4635929 online]
* Duffy, John.  ''A History of Public Health in New York City, 1625–1866'' (1968) the major scholarly study; covers sanitation, water supply, food safety, housing, schools, hospitals, diseases, medical care, and the progress of medicine. [https://www.russellsage.org/sites/default/files/HistPublicHealth2.pdf online] 
** Duffy, John. ''A History of Public Health in New York City, 1866-1966'' (1968) the major scholarly study; covers sanitation, water supply, food safety, housing, schools, hospitals, diseases, medical care, and the progress of medicine. [https://www.russellsage.org/sites/default/files/History-Public-Health.pdf online]
* {{cite journal | last1 = Duffy | first1 = John | year = 1974 | title = Nineteenth Century Public Health in New York and New Orleans: A Comparison | journal = Louisiana History | volume = 15 | issue = 4| pages = 325–37 | jstor = 4231424 | pmid = 11632467 }}
* Dwyer, Ellen. ''Homes for the mad : life inside two nineteenth-century asylums'' (1987) [https://archive.org/details/homesformadlifei0000dwye online] on upstate New York
* Ellis, John, and Stuart Galishoff. "Atlanta's Water Supply 1865–1918." in ''The Physical City'' (Routledge, 2013) pp.&nbsp;351–368.
* Ettling, John. ''The Germ of Laziness Rockefeller Philanthropy and Public Health in the New South'' (Harvard UP, 1981) on eradication of [[Hookworm]]
* Galishoff, Stuart. '' Newark: the nation's unhealthiest city, 1832-1895'' (1988). [https://archive.org/details/newarknationsunh0000gali online]
** Galishoff, " Public health in Newark, 1832-1918" (PhD Dissertation, New York University; ProQuest Dissertations & Theses,  1969. 7017269).      
** {{cite journal | last1 = Galishoff | first1 = Stuart | year = 1969 | title = Newark and the great influenza pandemic of 1918 | journal = Bulletin of the History of Medicine | volume = 43 | issue = 3| pages = 246–258 | jstor = 44449940 | pmid = 4895345 }}
* {{cite journal | last1 = Galishoff | first1 = Stuart | year = 1985 | title = Germs Know No Color Line: Black Health and Public Policy in Atlanta, 1900—1918 | journal = Journal of the History of Medicine and Allied Sciences | volume = 40 | issue = 1| pages = 22–41 | doi = 10.1093/jhmas/40.1.22 | jstor = 24633408 | pmid = 3881513 }}
* {{cite journal | last1 = Galishoff | first1 = Stuart | year = 1970 | title = Cholera in Newark, New Jersey | url = | journal = Journal of the History of Medicine and Allied Sciences | volume = 25 | issue = 4| pages = 438–448 | doi = 10.1093/jhmas/XXV.4.438 | pmid = 4919842 }}
* Galishoff, Stuart. "Triumph and failure: the American response to the urban water supply problem, 1860-1923." in ''Pollution and reform in American cities, 1870–1930'' (1980): 35–58.
* Howard, William Travis. ''Public health administration and the natural history of disease in Baltimore, Maryland, 1797-1920'' (1924)  [https://archive.org/details/publichealthadmi00howa_0/page/n4/mode/1up online], Comprehensive scholarly history.
*  Koehler, Gottfried. ''Annals of health and sanitation in Chicago'' (1919) [https://archive.org/details/annalsofhealthsa00koeh/page/n8/mode/1up online] excerpts from primary sources.
* Koslow, Jennifer. "Public Health" '' Encyclopedia of Chicago'' (2004) [http://www.encyclopedia.chicagohistory.org/pages/1020.html online]; with many other articles on public health in Chicago.
* McCandless, Peter. ''Moonlight, magnolias, and madness: Insanity in South Carolina from the colonial period to the progressive era'' (UNC Press Books, 2013).
* Molina, Natalia. ''Fit to be citizens?: public health and race in Los Angeles, 1879-1939'' (U of California Press, 2006) [https://books.google.com/books?hl=en&lr=&id=tERMpVQhPdgC&oi=fnd&pg=PR7&dq=Public+health+in+Los+Angeles.&ots=WBeZVQLz1A&sig=nTzJYCmm51tWVEeIIaYAszaP2a4 online].
*{{cite journal | pmid = 25423064 | doi=10.1097/PHH.0000000000000176 | volume=21 Suppl 1 | title=Big city urban health departments: catalysts in the crucible of population-based health | pmc=4243796 | year=2015 | journal=J Public Health Manag Pract | pages=S95-7  | last1 = Novick | first1 = LF | issue=Suppl 1 }}
*  O'Toole, Colleen K. ''The Search for Purity: A Retrospective Policy Analysis of the Decision to Chlorinate Cincinnati's Public Water Supply, 1890–1920'' (Garland, 1990)
* {{cite journal | last1 = Patterson | first1 = Andrea | year = 2009 | title = Germs and Jim Crow: The Impact of Microbiology on Public Health Policies in Progressive Era American South | journal = Journal of the History of Biology | volume = 42 | issue = 3| pages = 529–559 | doi = 10.1007/s10739-008-9164-x | jstor = 40271562 | pmid = 20027786 }}
* Rosenkrantz, Barbara Gutmann. ''Public health and the state: changing views in Massachusetts, 1842-1936'' (Harvard UP, 1972), a major study of the leading state; [https://archive.org/details/publichealthstat0000rose online]
* {{cite journal | last1 = Roth | first1 = Mitchel | year = 1997 | title = Cholera, community, and public health in Gold Rush Sacramento and San Francisco | journal = Pacific Historical Review | volume = 66 | issue = 4| pages = 527–551 | doi = 10.2307/3642236 | jstor = 3642236 }}

===Primary sources===
* Brieger, Gert H. ed. ''Medical America in the Nineteenth Century: Readings from the Literature'' (1972) [https://archive.org/details/medicalamericain00brie online]
* Bureau of the Census. ''Historical Statistics of the United States: Colonial times, to 1970.'' (1976) chapter B pp.&nbsp;65–86.. [https://books.google.com/books?hl=en&lr=&id=UWB02EGwFjoC&oi=fnd&pg=PR6&dq=%27%27Historical+Statistics+of+the+United+States:+Colonial+times,+to+1970.%27&ots=m7GqCneNIR&sig=-auBXk6tRVcPybffSixe3YdoKuY online]
* Cassedy, James H. "The Roots of American Sanitary Reform 1843–47: Seven Letters from John H. Griscom to Lemuel Shattuck" ''Journal of the History of Medicine and Allied Sciences'' (1975) 30#2 pp.&nbsp;136–147 [https://www.jstor.org/stable/24622560 online]
* Falk, I.S. et al. "The costs of medical care: A Summary of Investigations on the Economic Aspects of the Prevention and Care of Illness" (University of Chicago Press 1933), Publications of the Committee on the costs of medical care. #27).  [https://ia903407.us.archive.org/30/items/dli.ministry.11553/E05148_The_Costs_Of_Medical_Care_text.pdf online]
* Schneider, Dona, and David E. Lilienfeld, eds. ''Public Health: The Development of a Discipline, From the Age of Hippocrates to the Progressive Era'' (2008), Long excerpts from. 24 major documents. before 1920, from the United States and United Kingdom. [https://books.google.com/books?hl=en&lr=&id=4xv0scbWSAUC&oi=fnd&pg=PR9&dq=Schneider,+Dona,+and+David+E.+Lilienfeld&ots=j-GP3XunKj&sig=0SKekWocw8u5gK7nKY0CNp1kzZE excerpts]
**   ''Public health: the development of a discipline. Vol. 2 Twentieth century challenges'' (2011), covers 1920 to 2010.
* Warner, J.H. and J.A. Tighe,  eds. ''Major Problems in the History of American Medicine and Public Health'' (2001). short excerpts from over 100 scholarly and primary sources.
